author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
We Were Elected and Predestinated, Not Because We Were Going to Be Holy, But in Order that We Might Be So.
title: We Were Elected and Predestinated, Not Because We Were Going to Be Holy, But in Order that We Might Be So.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: It would be too tedious to argue about the several points.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But you see without doubt, you see with what evidence of apostolic declaration this grace is defended, in opposition to which human merits are set up, as if man should first give something for it to be recompensed to him again.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Therefore God chose us in Christ before the foundation of the world, predestinating us to the adoption of children, not because we were going to be of ourselves holy and immaculate, but He chose and predestinated us that we might be so.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Moreover, He did this according to the good pleasure of His will, so that nobody might glory concerning his own will, but about God’s will towards himself.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: He did this according to the riches of His grace, according to His good-will, which He purposed in His beloved Son; in whom we have obtained a share, being predestinated according to the purpose, not ours, but His, who worketh all things to such an extent as that He worketh in us to will also.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Moreover, He worketh according to the counsel of His will, that we may be to the praise of His glory.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For this reason it is that we cry that no one should glory in man, and, thus, not in himself; but whoever glorieth let him glory in the Lord, that he may be for the praise of His glory.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Because He Himself worketh according to His purpose that we may be to the praise of His glory, and, of course, holy and immaculate, for which purpose He called us, predestinating us before the foundation of the world.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Out of this, His purpose, is that special calling of the elect for whom He co-worketh with all things for good, because they are called according to His purpose, and “the gifts and calling of God are without repentance.”
